---
title: "Math Nomad"
page-layout: full
toc: false
title-block-banner: false
---

::: {.mn-hero}
::: {.mn-hero-copy}
<p class="mn-kicker">MATHEMATICS · EXPLORATION · TEACHING</p>

# Mathematics is a place to wander.

Math Nomad is a home for visual investigations, interactive tools, careful exposition and problems worth carrying with you.

::: {.mn-actions}
[Explore the interactives](explore/index.qmd){.btn .btn-primary .btn-lg}
[Read the first note](read/articles/binary-kolam-tiles/index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-dark .btn-lg}
:::
:::

::: {.mn-hero-art}
![A field of sixteen square kolam tiles, each with a different pattern of openings.](assets/kolam-field.svg){.img-fluid}
:::
:::

::: {.mn-intro .content-block}
## Three ways to enter

Choose an object to manipulate, an idea to understand, or an investigation to take into a classroom. The same mathematics often appears in all three forms.
:::

::: {.mn-three-up .content-block}
::: {.mn-path-card}
<span class="mn-card-number">01</span>

### Explore

Interact with a mathematical object. Make a conjecture, test it and notice what refuses to change.

[Open the laboratory →](explore/index.qmd)
:::

::: {.mn-path-card}
<span class="mn-card-number">02</span>

### Read

Follow an idea from experiment to definition, proof and computation without hiding the interesting turns.

[Browse the notes →](read/index.qmd)
:::

::: {.mn-path-card}
<span class="mn-card-number">03</span>

### Teach

Use investigations designed for discussion, group work and the productive pause before an answer appears.

[Find classroom material →](teach/index.qmd)
:::
:::

::: {.mn-feature-band}
::: {.content-block .mn-feature-grid}
::: {.mn-feature-copy}
<p class="mn-kicker">FEATURED EXPLORATION</p>

## Sixteen kolam tiles

Every four-bit word encodes a square tile. The bits record openings in the order east–north–west–south. Arrange all sixteen tiles so that neighbouring edges agree, the boundary closes and the drawing is connected.

The construction board, a sliding puzzle and an orbit challenge turn one small tile set into three distinct mathematical experiments.

[Enter the Kolam Lab](https://kolam-tile-builder.littleboy300.chatgpt.site){.btn .btn-light .btn-lg}
[Understand the encoding](read/articles/binary-kolam-tiles/index.qmd){.mn-text-link}
:::

::: {.mn-feature-facts}
<div><strong>16</strong><span>binary tiles</span></div>
<div><strong>4</strong><span>edge directions</span></div>
<div><strong>1</strong><span>connected drawing</span></div>
:::
:::
:::

::: {.mn-notes .content-block}
## Recently added

::: {.mn-note-row}
<div class="mn-note-mark">∿</div>
<div>

### [How four bits become a kolam](read/articles/binary-kolam-tiles/index.qmd)

A first look at the encoding, the local matching equations and the global connectedness condition.

</div>
<span class="mn-note-type">NOTE</span>
:::
:::
